faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Quick answers to common questions about jujutsu infinite codes and how they work.

faqItems.question: What do jujutsu infinite codes give you?
faqItems.answer: Most codes give you free Spins, which let you roll for Innate Techniques (special abilities inspired by Jujutsu Kaisen). Some codes also give Yen (in-game currency), XP boosts, or event-exclusive rewards.

faqItems.question: How often are new jujutsu infinite codes released?
faqItems.answer: New codes typically drop during game updates, player count milestones (like 1M visits), holidays, and special events. Check this page daily or follow the official Discord for the fastest updates.

faqItems.question: Do jujutsu infinite codes expire?
faqItems.answer: Yes. Most codes are time-limited and can expire without warning. Some last a few days, others last through an entire update cycle. Always redeem codes as soon as you find them.

faqItems.question: Can I use the same code twice?
faqItems.answer: No. Each code can only be redeemed once per Roblox account. If you see an 'already redeemed' error, it means you have already claimed that code.

faqItems.question: Why is my code not working?
faqItems.answer: The most common reasons are: the code has expired, you already redeemed it, there is a typo or extra space, or the game needs an update. Check our FAQ page for detailed troubleshooting steps.

faqItems.question: Where do I enter codes in Jujutsu Infinite?
faqItems.answer: Open the game, look for the gift box or Twitter/X icon on the side menu, tap it to open the codes panel, paste your code, and hit Redeem. See our redeem guide for step-by-step instructions.

paragraphs: Jujutsu Infinite is a popular Roblox game inspired by the anime Jujutsu Kaisen. Players can unlock powerful Innate Techniques — special abilities like Domain Expansion, Reverse Cursed Technique, and Cursed Speech — by using Spins. Codes are free gifts released by the developers that give you Spins and other rewards without spending Robux.

The most common reward is Spins. Each Spin gives you a random chance to roll a new Innate Technique. Higher-rarity techniques are much harder to get, so every free Spin counts. Some codes also give Yen (the in-game currency used for upgrades), XP boosts for faster leveling, or limited-time event items that are only available during special occasions.

subsections.h3: Why Are Codes Important for New Players?
paragraphs: If you are just starting Jujutsu Infinite, codes are the fastest way to get a strong Innate Technique without grinding. A good Innate can completely change your gameplay experience — some techniques let you dominate in PvP, while others are better for PvE boss fights. Redeeming all active codes on day one gives you the best possible head start.

sections.title: How to Redeem Codes in Jujutsu Infinite

paragraphs: Redeeming codes in Jujutsu Infinite takes less than 30 seconds. Open the game on Roblox, wait for your character to fully load, then look for the gift box icon or Twitter/X icon on the left side of your screen. Tap it to open the codes panel, paste your code exactly as shown, and press the Redeem button. Your reward will appear instantly.

1. Launch Jujutsu Infinite on Roblox (PC, mobile, or Xbox). 2. Wait until your character fully loads into the game world. 3. Find and tap the codes icon on the side menu (gift box or Twitter/X icon). 4. Type or paste the code exactly — codes are case-sensitive. 5. Press the Redeem or Claim button. 6. Check your inventory for the reward.

subsections.h3: Where to Put Codes in Jujutsu Infinite
paragraphs: The code input field is inside the game itself, not on the Roblox website or catalog. Look for a small icon on the left side of your screen after your character loads. If the icon is not visible, try rejoining the server or updating your Roblox app. The codes panel only appears after the game UI is fully loaded.

Always copy and paste codes instead of typing them manually to avoid typos. Make sure there are no extra spaces before or after the code. On mobile, watch out for auto-correct changing your code text. If a code fails on the first try, clear the input field completely and try again. Test a known working code first to make sure your session is connected properly.

sections.title: Why Are Jujutsu Infinite Codes Not Working?

paragraphs: There are four main reasons a jujutsu infinite code might not work: 1) The code has expired — developers can deactivate codes at any time. 2) You already redeemed it on your account — each code is one-time use. 3) There is a typo, extra space, or wrong capitalization. 4) Your game client is outdated and needs an update.
If a code was working yesterday but fails today, it has almost certainly expired. Check the expired codes list on our site to confirm before retrying.

subsections.h3: How Long Do Jujutsu Infinite Codes Last?
paragraphs: Code lifespans vary widely. Some codes last only a few days, especially those tied to specific events or milestones. Others can remain active for weeks or even months. There is no official expiration timer shown in-game, which is why we track and timestamp every code on this site. As a rule of thumb: redeem codes as soon as you find them. Do not save them for later.

sections.title: How to Get More Jujutsu Infinite Codes

paragraphs: The fastest way to find new codes is through official channels. Join the Jujutsu Infinite Discord server for real-time announcements. Follow the developers on Twitter/X and YouTube, where they often drop codes during update reveals and milestone celebrations. You can also join the official Roblox group for group-exclusive codes.

New jujutsu infinite codes are typically released during: game updates and patches, player count milestones (100K visits, 1M visits, etc.), holidays and seasonal events (Christmas, New Year, summer), collaboration events with other Roblox games, and developer live streams or YouTube videos. Major updates almost always come with at least one new code.
Bookmark this page and check back daily. We monitor all official sources and update our code list multiple times per day so you never miss a new drop.

Innate Techniques are the core abilities in Jujutsu Infinite, inspired by cursed techniques from the Jujutsu Kaisen anime. Each technique gives your character unique combat abilities. Some of the most sought-after techniques include Domain Expansion variants, Reverse Cursed Technique for healing, Cursed Speech for crowd control, and Limitless for defensive play. Rarer techniques are significantly more powerful in both PvP and PvE.

Every Spin gives you a random Innate Technique based on a rarity system. Common techniques are easy to get but weaker, while Mythic and Legendary techniques are extremely rare but game-changing. Free Spins from codes let you try for better techniques without spending Robux. The more Spins you have, the better your chances of landing a rare Innate — which is why redeeming every active code matters.

GameGuide.title: Complete Jujutsu Infinite Game Guide 2026 - Mechanics, Techniques & Strategies
GameGuide.description: Master Jujutsu Infinite with our comprehensive guide covering techniques, cursed energy, resource optimization, PvP strategies, and how to maximize code rewards.
GameGuide.heading: Complete Jujutsu Infinite Game Guide
GameGuide.subheading: Master game mechanics, optimize resources, and dominate PvP with this comprehensive 2026 guide

techniques.title: Innate Techniques System
techniques.intro: Jujutsu Infinite features a six-tier rarity system that determines your combat power and progression speed. Understanding this system is critical because codes primarily grant Spins, which are your gateway to obtaining powerful techniques.

common.name: Common
common.description: Basic starter abilities with simple movesets. Functional for early-game PvE but quickly outclassed. Drop rate: ~40%

uncommon.name: Uncommon
uncommon.description: Slightly improved abilities with better damage scaling. Still considered low-tier for mid-game content. Drop rate: ~30%

rare.name: Rare
rare.description: Solid mid-game techniques with multi-hit combos and better area damage. Viable through level 30-40. Drop rate: ~18%

epic.name: Epic
epic.description: Strong techniques with crowd control effects and respectable damage. Competitive in late-game PvP. Drop rate: ~8%

legendary.name: Legendary
legendary.description: Elite-tier techniques with devastating area damage, unique visual effects, and anime-inspired abilities. Drop rate: ~3%

mythic.name: Mythic
mythic.description: The pinnacle of power. Game-changing abilities with special mechanics, invincibility frames, and dominating damage. Drop rate: <1%

spinStrategy.title: Spin Strategy & Code Optimization
spinStrategy.content: Codes that grant Spins are the most valuable because each Spin is another chance at a Mythic technique. Save Spins for rate-up events (typically during major updates) when Legendary and Mythic drop rates increase by 50-100%. However, if you're a new player with only a Common technique, use your first 10-15 Spins immediately—the power difference is massive.

cursedEnergy.title: Cursed Energy (CE) System
cursedEnergy.intro: Your technique is only as strong as the Cursed Energy powering it. CE functions as both your mana pool and damage multiplier.

mechanics.title: How CE Works
mechanics.content: Base CE starts low and must be trained through specific exercises and quests. Higher CE capacity allows you to use more abilities in succession and increases overall damage output by 10-15% per 100 CE points. Training CE is time-intensive, which is why codes that grant XP boosts or training speed multipliers are incredibly valuable.

commonMistake.title: Common Mistake
commonMistake.content: Many new players focus solely on getting a Mythic technique while ignoring CE training, resulting in a powerful ability they can't use effectively. Balance your progression between technique acquisition and CE development.

leveling.title: Leveling & Progression System
leveling.intro: The level cap determines which content you can access and which techniques you can fully utilize.

early.title: Early Game (Levels 1-20)
early.content: Focus on basic combat training and story quests. Complete the tutorial fully to unlock all game systems. Use any codes for XP boosts immediately to accelerate through this phase.

mid.title: Mid Game (Levels 20-50)
mid.content: Unlocks PvP arenas, clan content, and harder boss raids. Start optimizing your technique choice and CE training. Join an active clan for bonus rewards.

late.title: Late Game (Level 50+)
late.content: Meta matters here—technique choice, CE optimization, and equipment become critical. Codes that grant XP boosts can cut leveling time by 30-40%.

yenManagement.title: Yen Management
yenManagement.intro: Yen is the primary currency for purchasing Spins, equipment, and consumables. Most efficient farming methods ranked:
methods: Daily quests: Guaranteed 300-500 Yen per day
High-level mob grinding: 200-400 Yen per 10 minutes
Code redemption: Varies, but can grant 5000+ Yen instantly
yenManagement.priority: Your first 10,000 Yen should go exclusively toward Spins. Once you have a Rare or better technique, start investing in equipment that boosts CE or damage output.

codePriority.title: Code Reward Prioritization
codePriority.intro: Not all codes are equal. Priority order:
list: Spin codes - Direct path to better techniques
XP boost codes - Faster leveling = faster progression
Yen codes - Can be converted to Spins
Cosmetic codes - Lowest priority unless you're a collector

comboSystem.title: Combo System
comboSystem.content: Every technique has a basic combo structure: light attacks build up to heavy attacks, which can be canceled into special abilities. Mastering animation canceling is the difference between average and elite players. Most techniques have a 3-4 hit optimal combo that maximizes damage per second while minimizing vulnerability windows.

pvpMeta.title: PvP Meta
pvpMeta.content: The PvP meta shifts with each major update, but some principles remain constant. Mobility techniques with dash or teleport abilities have inherent advantages. Techniques with crowd control (stuns, slows, knockbacks) are valuable for controlling engagement distance. Pure damage techniques need to be Legendary or Mythic tier to compete with utility-focused Epic techniques.

topTier.title: Top-Tier PvP Characteristics
features: High burst damage or sustained pressure

domainExpansion.title: Domain Expansion
domainExpansion.content: If your technique has a Domain Expansion (not all do), it functions as an ultimate ability with a long cooldown. Domains create a zone where your abilities are enhanced and opponents are debuffed. Timing is critical—use it to secure kills in PvP or burst down bosses in PvE. Never waste a Domain on regular mobs.

clanBenefits.title: Clan Benefits
clanBenefits.content: Joining an active clan provides multiple advantages. Clan quests grant bonus Yen and XP. Clan wars offer exclusive rewards including limited-time cosmetics and currency. Most importantly, active clans share code information immediately—you'll often hear about new codes in clan chat before they hit social media.

clanRaids.title: Clan Boss Raids
clanRaids.content: Weekly events that require coordination. These raids drop the highest Yen rewards in the game and occasionally drop exclusive equipment. Solo players miss out on 20-30% of available weekly rewards by not participating in clan content.

community.title: Community Resources
community.content: The Jujutsu Infinite community is active on Discord, Reddit, and YouTube. Official announcements come through the game's Discord server and the developer's Twitter/X account. For code hunting, following these channels is essential—codes often expire within 48-72 hours of release.

seasonalEvents.title: Seasonal Events
seasonalEvents.content: The developers run seasonal events tied to anime milestones, holidays, and player count achievements. These events introduce limited-time techniques that cannot be obtained through regular Spins. Event codes are especially valuable because they may grant exclusive cosmetics, event-only currency, or boosted Spin rates. Events typically last 1-2 weeks, and their codes expire when the event ends.

codePatterns.title: Code Release Patterns
codePatterns.intro: Through tracking hundreds of code releases, common patterns emerge. New codes typically drop during:
patterns: Player count milestones (every 100K likes or visits)
Anime episode premieres (when new Jujutsu Kaisen episodes air)
codePatterns.tip: The most generous code drops happen during major game updates when developers want to attract returning players.

faq.title: FAQ

BeginnerGuide.title: Jujutsu Infinite Beginner Guide 2026 - Complete Starter Tutorial
BeginnerGuide.description: Complete beginner's guide to Jujutsu Infinite. Learn how to start, redeem codes, get your first technique, level up fast, and avoid common mistakes.
BeginnerGuide.heading: Jujutsu Infinite Beginner Guide
BeginnerGuide.subheading: Your complete roadmap from installation to your first powerful technique

installation.title: Game Installation & Account Setup
installation.intro: Jujutsu Infinite is a Roblox game, so you'll need a Roblox account to play. If you already have Roblox installed, skip to finding the game.

findGame.title: Finding Jujutsu Infinite
findGame.content: Open Roblox and use the search bar at the top. Type 'Jujutsu Infinite' and look for the official game with the most players. The game icon typically features characters from Jujutsu Kaisen anime. Click the green Play button to launch the game.

accountSecurity.title: Account Security Tips
accountSecurity.content: Enable 2-Step Verification in your Roblox account settings. Never share your password with anyone claiming to offer free Robux or codes. All legitimate codes can be redeemed in-game for free—you never need to pay or provide account details.

firstLogin.title: First Login Checklist
firstLogin.content: When you first load into Jujutsu Infinite, you'll spawn in the starting area. Take a moment to adjust your graphics settings if the game is laggy. Check your ping in the top-right corner—anything under 200ms is playable. If you experience lag, try switching to a less crowded server.

tutorial.title: Complete the Tutorial
tutorial.intro: The in-game tutorial teaches you basic controls and combat mechanics. Don't skip it—you'll earn your first rewards and unlock essential game systems.

controls.title: Basic Controls
controls.content: WASD to move, Space to jump, Left Click for basic attacks. Your technique abilities are bound to Q, E, R, and F keys. Press M to open the menu where you'll redeem codes later. The tutorial will walk you through using these controls in combat.

firstBattles.title: Your First Battles
firstBattles.content: The tutorial pits you against weak cursed spirits. Focus on learning the combo system: light attacks (Left Click) build up to heavy attacks, which can be canceled into your technique abilities. Don't worry about dying—the tutorial is forgiving and will respawn you instantly.

rewards.title: Tutorial Rewards
rewards.content: Completing the tutorial grants you starter Yen (around 500-1000), some XP, and unlocks the Spin system. You'll also receive a random Common or Uncommon technique to start with. While these aren't powerful, they're enough to begin your journey.

codes.title: Redeem All Available Codes Immediately
codes.intro: This is the most important step for new players. Codes grant free Spins, Yen, and XP boosts that dramatically accelerate your early progression.

whyImportant.title: Why Codes Are Critical for Beginners
whyImportant.content: Without codes, you'd need to grind for hours to afford your first 10 Spins. With codes, you can get 20-30 Spins immediately, giving you multiple chances at a Rare or better technique. This single step can save you 5-10 hours of grinding.

howToRedeem.title: How to Redeem Codes
howToRedeem.content: Press M to open the menu. Look for a Codes or Gift icon (usually on the left side). Click it to open the code redemption panel. Type or paste the code exactly as shown—codes are case-sensitive. Click Redeem and check your inventory for rewards.

priority.title: Code Redemption Priority
priority.content: Redeem codes in this order: 1) Spin codes first (direct path to better techniques), 2) Yen codes second (can buy more Spins), 3) XP boost codes third (faster leveling), 4) Cosmetic codes last (purely visual).

whereToFind.title: Where to Find Active Codes
whereToFind.content: Check our Live Codes page for all currently working codes. We update it daily with automatic verification. Codes typically expire within 48-72 hours, so check back frequently. Join the official Discord for instant notifications when new codes drop.

firstSpin.title: Your First Spin Strategy
firstSpin.intro: Now that you have Spins from codes, it's time to get a proper technique. Your strategy depends on what you rolled from the tutorial.

whenToSpin.title: When to Use Your Spins
whenToSpin.content: If you have a Common or Uncommon technique from the tutorial, use all your Spins immediately. Don't wait for rate-up events—the power difference between Common and Rare is so massive that it's worth spinning now. If you somehow got a Rare or better from the tutorial, you can save some Spins for future events.

whatToAimFor.title: What Techniques to Aim For
whatToAimFor.content: Your goal is to get at least a Rare technique. Rare techniques have multi-hit combos and decent damage that will carry you to level 30-40. If you get an Epic or Legendary, you're set for the entire early and mid-game. Mythic techniques are extremely rare (<1% drop rate) so don't expect one, but if you get lucky, you've won the lottery.

badLuck.title: What If You Get Bad Luck?
badLuck.content: If you use all your Spins and still have Common/Uncommon, don't panic. Focus on completing daily quests to earn more Yen, then buy more Spins. The game is designed to eventually give you something decent. Meanwhile, even a Common technique can clear early-game content if you level up your Cursed Energy.

goodLuck.title: If You Get a Good Technique
goodLuck.content: Congratulations! Now focus on learning your technique's combo patterns. Go to the training area (usually marked on the map) and practice your Q-E-R-F abilities. Learn which abilities combo into each other and which have the longest range. This knowledge is more valuable than the technique itself.

leveling.title: Level 1-10 Progression Path
leveling.intro: Your first 10 levels teach you the game's core loop: quest, fight, level up, get stronger. This section is designed to be fast—you should hit level 10 in 30-60 minutes.

bestQuests.title: Best Quests for New Players
bestQuests.content: Focus on main story quests (marked with yellow exclamation marks). These give the most XP and unlock new areas. Side quests (white exclamation marks) are optional but give decent Yen. Avoid daily quests until level 10—they're designed for higher-level players.

farmingSpots.title: Best Farming Spots (Levels 1-10)
farmingSpots.content: The starting area has weak cursed spirits that give 20-50 XP each. Stay here until level 5. At level 5, move to the Forest area (follow the main quest). Enemies here give 80-150 XP. At level 8, you can start doing the Cave dungeon for 200+ XP per enemy.

xpBoosts.title: Using XP Boost Codes
xpBoosts.content: If you redeemed XP boost codes earlier, activate them now. XP boosts typically last 30-60 minutes and increase XP gain by 50-100%. Use them while actively grinding, not while AFK. The boost timer only counts down while you're in-game.

whenPvP.title: When to Start PvP
whenPvP.content: Don't enter PvP until level 15-20 minimum. PvP matchmaking doesn't have strict level brackets, so you'll face players with Legendary techniques and maxed Cursed Energy. Focus on PvE until you have a Rare+ technique and understand your combos.

community.title: Join Community & Clan
community.intro: Jujutsu Infinite is more fun and rewarding with others. Joining a clan and community gives you access to exclusive content, faster code notifications, and helpful players.

discord.title: Official Discord Server
discord.content: The official Jujutsu Infinite Discord is where developers announce new codes, updates, and events. Join it immediately and enable notifications for the #codes channel. You'll get new codes within minutes of release, often before they spread to other platforms.

clans.title: Finding a Good Clan
clans.content: Clans unlock at level 10. Look for clans marked as 'Beginner Friendly' or 'Active Daily'. Avoid clans that require high levels or specific techniques—you want a clan that helps new players. Good clans share codes in clan chat, run beginner-friendly raids, and answer questions.

clanBenefits.title: Clan Benefits for Beginners
clanBenefits.content: Clan quests give 50% more Yen than solo quests. Clan members can help you with difficult bosses. Most importantly, active clans share new codes immediately—you'll often get codes 30-60 minutes before they hit Reddit or YouTube.

youtube.title: YouTube & Content Creators
youtube.content: Several YouTubers specialize in Jujutsu Infinite content. They post code videos, technique showcases, and farming guides. Subscribe to 2-3 popular creators for regular updates. However, always verify codes on our site first—YouTube videos can be outdated.

mistakes.title: Common Beginner Mistakes to Avoid
mistakes.intro: Learn from others' mistakes. Avoid these common pitfalls that slow down new players.

list.why: The #1 mistake. Codes can give you 20-30 free Spins worth 3000-4500 Yen. That's 5-10 hours of grinding. Always check for codes before you start grinding.
Cosmetics don't make you stronger. Your first 10,000 Yen should go exclusively to Spins. Once you have a Rare+ technique, then you can buy cosmetics.
PvP has no level brackets. You'll face level 50+ players with Mythic techniques. Focus on PvE until level 20 minimum, preferably level 30.
Your technique is only as strong as your CE. Even a Mythic technique does poor damage with low CE. Balance technique acquisition with CE training.
Clans give 50% more rewards and share codes instantly. You're missing out on 20-30% of available content by not joining a clan.
If you have a Common technique, use your Spins now. The power spike from Common to Rare is massive and will accelerate all your other progression.

tips.title: Pro Tips for Fast Progression
tips.intro: These tips will help you progress faster than 90% of new players.

list.why: New codes drop 2-3 times per week. Bookmark our Live Codes page and check it every morning. Set a phone reminder if needed.
Once you hit level 10, daily quests give guaranteed Yen and XP. They reset every 24 hours. Completing them takes 10-15 minutes and gives 500-800 Yen.
Canceling attack animations into abilities increases your DPS by 30-40%. Practice in the training area until it becomes muscle memory.
Boss raids give 500-1000 Yen per completion. Join clan raids even if you're low level—your clan will carry you and you still get full rewards.

list.tip: Use XP Boosts During Grind Sessions
list.why: Don't waste XP boosts while doing quests or AFK. Activate them when you're about to grind mobs for 30-60 minutes straight.
Once you have a Rare+ technique, save 10-15 Spins for rate-up events. During events, Legendary/Mythic drop rates double.

nextSteps.title: What to Do After Level 10
nextSteps.intro: You've completed the beginner phase. Here's what to focus on next:
goals: Reach level 20 to unlock more content and harder quests
Train your Cursed Energy to at least 500 points
Join an active clan and participate in clan raids
Start learning PvP basics in casual matches
Save Yen for the next rate-up event if you have a Rare+ technique
Check codes daily—new codes can give you another 10-20 Spins
